derivation: Hand-authored representative strategy for NibbitsNormal mid-combat probe. NOT oracle-derived. Two Nibbits: slot 1 = front Nibbit (IsFront=true; starts SLICE_MOVE via upstream ConditionalBranchState.IsFront predicate); slot 2 = back Nibbit (IsFront=false; starts HISS_MOVE via !IsFront predicate). Q1 port NibbitsNormal.GenerateMonstersWithMoves returns (SLICE_MOVE, HISS_MOVE) per slot — confirmed no V1 divergence. Per V12 baked: target back Nibbit (slot 2; HISS source) first to halt Strength stacking. Front rotation after initial: SLICE→HISS→BUTT→...; Back rotation after initial: HISS→BUTT→SLICE→... Turn 1: front=SLICE(attack 6+5self-block), back=HISS(Strength+2); target back to cut Strength early. Turn 2: front=HISS(Strength+2), back=BUTT(attack 12+2str=14); Defend to absorb back BUTT. Turn 3: front=BUTT(attack 12+2str=14), back=SLICE(attack 6+2str=8+5self-block); Defend for front BUTT pressure. Turn 4: front=SLICE, back=HISS; strike back to kill (2 strikes/turn, ~4 turns to ~42-46HP back Nibbit). Once back dead (dead-creature targeting is a no-op per T3), shift to front. NibbitsNormal has 2-Nibbit damage pressure; 10-turn target met assuming typical HP range. target_creature_id=1 (front), =2 (back). Per V2 flex: if Strength escalation makes ≥10 turns infeasible, PASS-DEFERRED-at-<10-turns accepted (wave-46/A.3 precedent). In practice at Ascension 0 HISS+2str/proc this encounter is tractable.
